purpose: Workstream C fixtures are a two-sided proof: the fixture must be demonstrably RED against pre-fix main, then flipped to enforced-green by the fix bead. This file records the red half so the flip is auditable rather than asserted.

expected_failure.flip_action: delete both #[ignore] attributes; no assertion change is permitted

red_run.result: FAILED. 0 passed; 2 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 3 filtered out

failures.panic_message: the `missing token` error is the one place a stuck integrator is guaranteed to look, so it must spell out params._meta["oraclemcp/initToken"]; got: stdio init token missing from initialize request
failures.root_cause: InitTokenError::Missing (crates/oraclemcp-core/src/init_token.rs) names neither the _meta key nor the JSON path it read.

failures.panic_message: the literal key `oraclemcp/initToken` must appear in at least one client-facing source.
failures.root_cause: Zero hits for the string oraclemcp/initToken across README.md, oraclemcp.example.toml, robot_docs.rs, and docs/ outside docs/plan/. The key exists only in Rust source, and it contains a slash, so it cannot be guessed.

green_half.result: ok. 3 passed; 0 failed; 2 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out
green_half.note: The extraction contract itself works and is now pinned against a hand-written frame. c2_literal_frame_is_a_mismatch_not_a_miss_against_a_different_token is the load-bearing assertion: Mismatch (not Missing) proves the committed literal path still matches the server's key, which is exactly what a frame built from INIT_TOKEN_META_KEY can never prove.

external_mint.note: Frames are committed as opaque one-line JSON. The file never imports INIT_TOKEN_META_KEY; renaming that constant must turn the green half red.
